beats.narration: Unlike a gamma source, an X ray tube makes radiation only while energized. Power off, hazard off. Let's build the machine behind the beam.

payload.title: X-RAYS ON DEMAND
payload.sub: Inside the industrial X-ray tube — 500 keV and less.

beats.narration: A heated filament boils off electrons. High voltage slams them into the tungsten target. About one percent becomes X rays; the rest is heat.

payload.heading: Electrons in, X-rays out
payload.note: ONLY ≈1% BECOMES X-RAYS — THE REST IS HEAT

beats.narration: Milliamps set quantity, how many X rays. Kilovolts set quality, how penetrating. The timer sets how long the beam stays on.

tiles.s: How long the beam is on

beats.narration: About ninety nine percent becomes heat. Warm a cold tube in steps, respect the duty cycle. Push past it and you crack the target.
So: warm it up. Set factors. Mind the heat. And filter the beam: soft rays add scatter and dose, not information.

payload.sub: And filter the beam — soft rays add scatter, not information.
